adapterBoundary.policy: Thin host adapters can be the product surface; flag them as keep-with-proof instead of deletion candidates.

gainHonesty.policy: ShipGuard reports benchmark impact separately and does not invent per-repo line, token, cost, or time savings.

hardwareCalibration.policy: Hardware, sensors, clocks, and physical devices need calibration/tuning proof before simplification.

oneRunnableCheck.policy: Non-trivial new logic should leave one smallest runnable check; trivial one-liners do not need ceremony.

requestedExplanation.policy: Explicitly requested reports, walkthroughs, or phase notes are not clutter.

decisions.firstExperiment: Search the changed call sites and delete the wrapper only if it is private and behavior-neutral.
decisions.stopCondition: Stop if call sites depend on naming, compatibility, logging, typing, or behavior policy.
decisions.validationRoute: Run the smallest formatter behavior check plus git diff --check.

decisions.firstExperiment: Try URLComponents or URLQueryItem at the changed call site before adding parser code.
decisions.stopCondition: Stop if repeated keys, empty values, encoding, or malformed input behavior changes.
decisions.validationRoute: Run one focused query-string behavior check plus git diff --check.

decisions.firstExperiment: Treat this as a keep-with-proof boundary before any deletion or simplification.
decisions.stopCondition: Stop if the only evidence is less-code pressure without behavior proof.
decisions.validationRoute: Run focused permission-state behavior proof before changing this branch.

decisions.firstExperiment: Add or identify one smallest runnable route-selection check before merging the new branch.
decisions.stopCondition: Stop if the diff has no runnable proof signal for the changed non-trivial logic.
decisions.validationRoute: Run the focused route-selection test plus git diff --check.

decisions.firstExperiment: Review the matching same-diff test before adding any duplicate test ceremony.
decisions.stopCondition: Stop if the changed same-diff test already proves the new branch and no Lean cleanup remains.
decisions.validationRoute: Run Tests/SyntheticLeanReviewTests/SelectionPolicyTests.swift or the equivalent focused lane.

decisions.firstExperiment: Run the changed focused test and confirm it covers the changed selection branch.
decisions.stopCondition: Stop if the test is unrelated to the changed logic.
decisions.validationRoute: Run the focused test lane before broader validation.

decisions.firstExperiment: Keep this unrelated smoke test visible as evidence that proof signals are not global.
decisions.stopCondition: Stop if a maintainer tries to count this unrelated test as proof for a different changed file.
decisions.validationRoute: Run only if this separate smoke path matters to the task.

deleteOrSimplifyList.firstExperiment: Search the changed call sites and delete the wrapper only if it is private and behavior-neutral.
deleteOrSimplifyList.stopCondition: Stop if call sites depend on naming, compatibility, logging, typing, or behavior policy.
deleteOrSimplifyList.validationRoute: Run the smallest formatter behavior check plus git diff --check.

deleteOrSimplifyList.firstExperiment: Try URLComponents or URLQueryItem at the changed call site before adding parser code.
deleteOrSimplifyList.stopCondition: Stop if repeated keys, empty values, encoding, or malformed input behavior changes.
deleteOrSimplifyList.validationRoute: Run one focused query-string behavior check plus git diff --check.

currentDiffDecisionMap.inventoryBoundary: This report is built only from the supplied unified diff; it does not scan the whole repo or claim a whole-repo inventory.
nonClaims: Does not prove whole-repo inventory coverage.
Does not prove benchmark savings, token savings, cost savings, or time savings.
Does not authorize private target-app edits from ShipGuard product-QA runs.

evidence.snippet: func formatTitle(_ value: String) -> String { value.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines) }

findings.proofGuidance: Search call sites and keep one small check if behavior is non-trivial.
findings.recommendation: Inline or delete this wrapper unless it adds policy, naming, compatibility, logging, typing, or test value.

findings.proofGuidance: Add one check for repeated keys, empty values, encoding, and malformed input before replacing behavior.
findings.recommendation: Prefer URLComponents or URLQueryItem when runtime support allows it.

findings.proofGuidance: Attach focused before/after tests for trust-boundary, data-loss, security, permission, or accessibility behavior.
findings.recommendation: Less code is not the goal in this file until behavior proof exists.

findings.proofGuidance: Add one focused route-selection check or point to the changed test that covers this branch before merge.
findings.recommendation: Leave one smallest runnable check for the new routing branch instead of treating tests as optional ceremony.

findings.proofGuidance: Review Tests/SyntheticLeanReviewTests/SelectionPolicyTests.swift, then run the focused test before merge.
findings.recommendation: Do not add duplicate test ceremony before checking whether the same-diff proof signal already covers this logic.

expectedAssertions: report-quality preserves the actionability question in JSON and Markdown
report-quality emits a fixtureCandidates entry for the public synthetic case
the fixture keeps scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly explicit
shareable output contains no local absolute paths or private app identifiers
the fixture exposes currentDiffDecisionMap.scope as current-diff-only
the fixture exposes currentDiffDecisionMap decisions and the delete/simplify subset
the Markdown exposes Current Diff Decision Map plus the whole-repo non-claim and lean-audit fallback
the fixture exposes runnableCheckReview.missingProofFindings for non-trivial logic without proof
the fixture exposes runnableCheckReview.sameDiffProofFindings for non-trivial logic with a matching same-diff proof signal
the fixture exposes proofSignalCalibration counts for missing proof and same-diff proof without treating unrelated tests as global proof
the fixture exposes proofSignalMatching rows plus unmatched proof signals so unrelated tests are not global proof
the Markdown exposes Runnable Check Review, Missing Runnable Checks, Same-Diff Proof Signals, and the non-ceremony boundary
the Markdown exposes Proof Signal Matching and Unmatched Proof Signals
fixtureCandidate.privateDataPolicy: This materialized starter is synthetic. Use source reports only to choose report shape; do not copy private app code, local paths, screenshots, app-specific identifiers, or proprietary text.

promotion.promotionPolicy: Promotion is explicit maintainer work. ShipGuard writes starter files only to the requested output directory and does not auto-copy them into the repository.
reviewChecklist: Confirm the fixture is synthetic and public-safe.
Confirm no private app code, screenshots, local paths, tokens, app identifiers, or proprietary text were copied.
Confirm scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly remain explicit.
Confirm scoring the promoted fixture does not emit recursive fixtureCandidates.
Register the fixture in validation only after the report-quality check passes.

fixtureCandidate.publicFixtureRecipe: Create a minimal synthetic ShipGuard report pair that reproduces this report-quality question without private app code, local paths, screenshots, customer data, app names, or proprietary source snippets.
fixtureCandidate.sourceQuestion: Does proofSignalCalibration distinguish missing runnable checks from same-diff proof signals?

leanMode.intent: Use the proof ladder before adding or deleting code.
leanMode.policy: Use full mode for current-diff review when proof boundaries matter.

nextActions: Start with currentDiffDecisionMap.deleteOrSimplifyList[0] before reading whole-repo inventory.
Use shipguard lean audit only when a whole-repo Lean inventory is actually needed.
Do not convert this ShipGuard product-QA fixture into target-app implementation work.

actionGroups.stopCondition: Stop if call sites depend on naming, compatibility, logging, typing, or behavior policy.

actionGroups.validationRoute: Run the focused route-selection test plus git diff --check.

blockedByProof.action: Leave one smallest runnable route-selection check.
blockedByProof.proofRequired: Add one focused route-selection check or point to the changed test that covers this branch before merge.

deleteList.action: Inline or delete this wrapper unless it adds policy.
deleteList.proofRequired: Search call sites and keep one small check if behavior is non-trivial.

keepList.reason: Less-code pressure is not enough for permission-state branches.

precisionReview.principle: The best ShipGuard code is the code that proves it needs to exist.

simplifyFirst.action: Prefer URLComponents or URLQueryItem when runtime support allows it.
simplifyFirst.proofRequired: Add one check for repeated keys, empty values, encoding, and malformed input.

topActions.stopCondition: Stop if call sites depend on naming, compatibility, logging, typing, or behavior policy.

proofSignalCalibration.policy: Lean Review should distinguish no proof signal from same-diff proof signal. Same-diff tests still need human relevance review, but they should not produce duplicate missing-check ceremony.

proofSignalMatching.nonGlobalProofBoundary: Unrelated or unmatched proof signals are listed separately and do not satisfy missing proof for other changed files; same-diff proof is not treated as global proof.
proofSignalMatching.policy: Same-diff proof is file-scoped. A changed test or assertion satisfies a changed code file only when ShipGuard can match it by same file, path stem, or meaningful path tokens.

reportQualityQuestions: Does proofSignalCalibration distinguish missing runnable checks from same-diff proof signals?

missingProofFindings.proofGuidance: Add one focused route-selection check or point to the changed test that covers this branch before merge.
missingProofFindings.recommendation: Leave one smallest runnable check for the new routing branch instead of treating tests as optional ceremony.

runnableCheckReview.nonCeremonyBoundary: If the same diff already changes a focused test, XCTest, assertion, or explicit check signal, Lean Review records that same-diff proof signal instead of asking for duplicate test ceremony. The maintainer still needs to review relevance and run the focused check.
runnableCheckReview.policy: Non-trivial branch, loop, parser, and collection logic should leave one smallest runnable check.
proofToReview: For missing-proof rows, add or identify the smallest runnable check that covers the changed behavior.
For same-diff proof rows, run the changed check and confirm it exercises the changed non-trivial logic.

sameDiffProofFindings.proofGuidance: Review Tests/SyntheticLeanReviewTests/SelectionPolicyTests.swift, then run the focused test before merge.
sameDiffProofFindings.recommendation: Do not add duplicate test ceremony before checking whether the same-diff proof signal already covers this logic.

scanScope.note: Synthetic fixture; no private source tree was scanned.

scopeBoundary.purpose: Synthetic public fixture for ShipGuard report-quality behavior.

sourceInfluence.boundary: ShipGuard implements a native diff review for unnecessary code. It does not vendor Ponytail code.
sourceInfluence.name: Ponytail
